pulse: The Consumer sector trades at a median EV/EBITDA of 15.8x and median P/E of 28.3x, which is modestly above the broader S&P 500 forward P/E of 21.6x, reflecting a growth profile of just 4.6% in revenue. Notably, Walmart (WMT) exhibits a rich valuation at 24.5x EV/EBITDA and a trailing P/E of 46.9x despite a subpar profit margin of 3.1%, highlighting a significant valuation spread compared to lower-valued players like Target (TGT) at 8.5x EV/EBITDA and 13.8x P/E. As cyclicals gain traction, investors should closely monitor how shifting consumer spending impacts top-line growth for companies with elevated valuations, as sector-wide margins may continue to compress amidst competitive pressures.
